title: New U.S. recommendation on hepatitis B vaccine will have dire consequences, studies project
summary: Policy that recommends offering hep B vaccine at birth only to babies perceived to be at risk will lead to increased numbers of infected infants, new studies project.

title: Supreme Court grapples with multibillion-dollar wave of lawsuits over Roundup cancer claims
summary: The Supreme Court seemed divided Monday over whether to block thousands of lawsuits alleging the maker of the weedkiller Roundup failed to warn people it could cause cancer.

title: STAT+: Erasca touts strong, though preliminary, results in trial of pancreatic and lung cancer therapy
summary: The drugmaker Erasca said results on its experimental pill in a trial of pancreatic cancer and non-small cell lung cancer exceeded its expectations.

title: Lilly to buy startup Ajax in bid for a better JAK drug

title: STAT+: Oruka’s long-acting psoriasis therapy posts strong results in mid-stage study
summary: A long-acting injectable treatment for plaque psoriasis helped 63% of patients achieve complete skin clearance in a clinical trial, its maker, Oruka Therapeutics, reported.

title: NIH-funded research lags in reporting sex differences, new study finds
summary: Fewer than half of papers published by NIH-funded researchers analyze or report their data by sex, a new study found.

title: Drugmaker AbbVie chooses North Carolina for $1.4B manufacturing campus
summary: The 185-acre site will be the company’s largest single investment to date, with a focus on small-volume parenteral products like vials and prefilled syringes.

title: Roche, facing biosimilar threats, puts faith in new cancer and obesity drugs

title: Sanofi posts upbeat sales as R&D pressure builds
